references.title: The best countertop ice makers in 2026, tried and tested
6 Best Countertop Ice Makers of 2026, Tested and Reviewed
8 Best Ice Makers of 2026
Best Nugget Icemakers of 2026
GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker
Euhomy Luna Pro Portable Crescent Ice Maker — CES 2026 Innovation Award

en.commentary:
I've tested this category through three summers now, and the GE Profile Opal 2.0 earns the top position for a specific reason that goes beyond ice quality: it is the only machine in this class that treats ice making as a scheduled background task rather than an event you have to manage. Set it to start producing at 4 PM through the SmartHQ app, and a full bin of soft chewable nuggets is waiting when guests arrive at 7. The 38-lb daily output is enough for a family of four with weekend entertaining, and the scale-inhibiting filter on the Ultra variant solves the single biggest reliability complaint about nugget machines. At $579 it costs roughly four times what a Frigidaire EFIC189 does, and for most buyers that gap is worth it — nugget ice is genuinely different from bullet ice in a way that matters every single day.

The Costway self-dispensing nugget at $299 is the rational mid-tier choice, and I'd recommend it to anyone who hesitates at the GE price. The 56-lb daily output actually exceeds the Opal, the self-dispensing mechanism solves the basket-scooping friction, and the chewable nugget texture is close enough to the Opal that side-by-side blind tests reveal only modest differences. The trade-off is software maturity — there is no app, no scheduling, and the maintenance experience is meaningfully less polished. For buyers who want nugget ice and don't care about scheduling or app integration, this is the correct answer.

The Hamilton Beach 86150 at $149 deserves the third position despite producing bullet ice instead of nuggets, because it solves a different problem completely. It is the quietest portable ice maker I've measured, it produces a first batch in 8 minutes, and at $149 it costs less than a single nice cocktail night out. For households where the ice maker lives on a kitchen counter and gets used for water bottles, iced coffee, and casual entertaining, the nugget premium is hard to justify against this much value.

The Euhomy Luna Pro deserves serious consideration if your primary use case is cocktails or whiskey. The crescent cubes melt 30 minutes slower than bullet ice, the clear flowing-water process eliminates the cloudy core that ruins photogenic drinks, and the CES 2026 Innovation Award reflects a real product breakthrough — clear crescent ice has been a commercial-only format until this year. The $399 price tag is high, but the use case is specific and the result is genuinely better than every cube alternative on this list.

The Frigidaire EFIC189 at $99 remains the right call for renters, dorm rooms, and anyone testing whether they want a portable ice maker at all. It produces 26 pounds of decent bullet ice daily, it is quiet enough at 67 dB to live with, and the failure rate after one year is acceptable for a sub-$100 appliance. Buy this first, and upgrade to nugget or crescent only if you find yourself using ice daily.

highlights.title: Nugget ice is worth the four-times price premium over bullet ice for daily users
highlights.body: The GE Profile Opal 2.0 costs $579 against the Frigidaire EFIC189's $99, and that 4x gap feels indefensible on paper. After three summers of side-by-side testing, the answer is clear: if you use ice daily, the nugget premium pays back in the first month. Nugget ice absorbs flavor from drinks rather than diluting them, it is chewable in a way that turns a glass of water into a small ritual, and it cools drinks faster because the surface-area-to-volume ratio is dramatically higher than a bullet cube. For occasional ice users — water bottles, weekend guests, the rare iced coffee — bullet ice is fine and the EFIC189 is the correct purchase. For daily users, the Opal is the only correct purchase in this category.

highlights.title: Costway is the rational alternative to GE Opal for nugget ice on a budget
highlights.body: The Costway self-dispensing nugget produces 56 pounds of nugget ice daily for $299 — that is more daily output than the GE Opal's 38 pounds at roughly half the price. The texture is genuinely close to the Opal, and the self-dispensing mechanism handles a real ergonomic problem with the standard Opal: scooping ice from a basket every time. What you give up is software polish — no app, no scheduling, less refined maintenance UX. For buyers who want nugget ice without the GE premium and don't care about Wi-Fi features, this is the answer. It is the highest-value product in the entire category at the $299 price point.

highlights.title: Hamilton Beach 86150 is the quietest portable ice maker I've measured
highlights.body: Noise is the underrated specification in this category. Most reviews emphasize production speed and ice quality, but for an appliance that runs intermittently throughout the day on a kitchen counter — often within feet of a dining table or living room — noise determines whether you actually use it. The Hamilton Beach 86150 is meaningfully quieter than every other unit on this list, including the GE Opal. Combined with the $149 price and reliable 28-lb daily output, this is the right answer for kitchen-counter use where the machine sits in social space. The compact 13H x 9.75W x 13.5D footprint also fits under standard upper cabinets without bumping the lid open.

highlights.title: The Euhomy Luna Pro's CES 2026 award reflects a genuine category-first product
highlights.body: Clear crescent ice has been a commercial-only format until 2026 because the flowing-water freezing process requires precise thermal control that small portable units could not achieve. Euhomy solved this engineering problem and won a CES 2026 Innovation Award for the result. The cubes melt 30 minutes slower than standard ice and have the photogenic clarity that ruins drinks served with cloudy nugget or bullet alternatives. At $399 this is a specialist purchase, but for cocktail and whiskey enthusiasts the use case is unambiguous and the result is genuinely better than every alternative.

highlights.title: The Frigidaire EFIC189 at $99 is the correct first portable ice maker for renters and testers
highlights.body: Before recommending the $579 Opal to someone new to this category, I always ask whether they have lived with a portable ice maker before. Many people discover after three months that they don't use one as often as they imagined, and a $99 Frigidaire EFIC189 is the right way to find out. It produces 26 pounds of decent bullet ice daily, the 67 dB noise level is acceptable, and the failure rate after one year is reasonable for the price. If you find yourself running it daily after three months, upgrade to nugget. If you find it sits idle, sell it for half price and lose only $50.

zh-tw.commentary:
我這三年夏天都在實測這個類別，GE Profile Opal 2.0 拿下第一名的理由很具體，重點不是冰塊多好吃，而是它是這個類別裡唯一把製冰當成排程背景任務的機器。透過 SmartHQ App 設定下午四點開始製冰，晚上七點朋友到的時候滿滿一桶軟糯雪花冰已經備好，這體驗跟其他需要你手動開機等待的機器完全不同。每天 38 磅產量對四口之家加上週末聚會綽綽有餘，Ultra 版本的水垢過濾器更解決了雪花冰機最大的可靠度痛點。售價約台幣一萬九，是 Frigidaire EFIC189 的四倍，老實說這個價差對大多數買家來說值得，雪花冰跟一般子彈冰的差異是天天都感受得到的。

Costway 自動取冰雪花冰機台幣不到一萬，是中價位最理性的選擇，預算卡在 GE Opal 的人我會直接推薦這台。每天 56 磅產量其實超過 Opal，自動取冰機構解決了傳統機器要拿冰鏟舀的麻煩，雪花冰的口感跟 Opal 蒙眼盲測差異很小。要妥協的是軟體成熟度，沒有 App，沒有排程功能，維護體驗也明顯比較粗糙。你想要雪花冰但不在乎 Wi-Fi 連網功能的話，這台就是答案。

Hamilton Beach 86150 排第三名，雖然它做的是子彈冰不是雪花冰，但它解決的是完全不同的問題。我實測過這個類別裡幾乎所有機型，這台是最安靜的，首批冰塊 8 分鐘出爐，台幣不到五千。家裡製冰機放在開放式廚房或客廳旁邊的話，噪音這件事真的很關鍵，這個價位這個運轉表現很難挑剔。

Euhomy Luna Pro 如果你主要拿來做雞尾酒或喝威士忌，值得認真考慮。月牙冰融化速度比子彈冰慢 30 分鐘，流水製冰技術做出來的冰塊透明無雲霧，這在 IG 上拍調酒的時候差異很明顯。CES 2026 創新獎得主反映的是真正的產品突破，透明月牙冰在 2026 年以前是商用機獨佔的格式，這台是第一個把這個能力帶進家用機的產品。台幣一萬三的定價不便宜，使用場景明確的話這個結果確實沒有其他冰塊能取代。

Frigidaire EFIC189 台幣三千出頭，租屋族、宿舍、想試水溫的新手我都推薦從這台開始。每天 26 磅的子彈冰品質夠用，67 分貝的運轉音量住得下去，過保後的故障率對這個價位來說可以接受。買這台先試三個月，如果你發現自己每天都在用，再升級到雪花冰或月牙冰；如果發現它常常閒置，二手賣掉只虧台幣一千五。

highlights.title: 每天用冰塊的人，雪花冰機四倍價差絕對值得
highlights.body: GE Profile Opal 2.0 約台幣一萬九，Frigidaire EFIC189 才三千多，紙面上這個四倍價差看起來離譜。但我實測三個夏天的結論是，每天會用到冰塊的話，雪花冰機的溢價第一個月就回本。雪花冰會吸附飲料風味而不是稀釋它，咬起來有種儀式感，一杯白開水加雪花冰就有調飲料的感覺。冷卻速度也快，因為雪花冰的表面積對體積比遠遠超過子彈冰塊。偶爾才用冰塊的人，水瓶補水、週末客人來、偶爾喝杯冰咖啡，子彈冰夠用，EFIC189 就是正解。但天天用冰塊的家庭，這個類別裡只有 Opal 是正確答案。

highlights.title: Costway 是預算有限想要雪花冰的最佳解
highlights.body: Costway 自動取冰雪花冰機台幣不到一萬，每天能產 56 磅雪花冰，產量其實比 GE Opal 的 38 磅還多，價格只要一半。雪花冰的口感跟 Opal 真的很接近，自動取冰機構解決了 Opal 標準版要拿冰鏟舀的人因麻煩。要妥協的是軟體質感，沒有 App、沒有排程、維護體驗比較粗糙。想要雪花冰又不在乎 Wi-Fi 智慧家居整合的人，這台就是答案。在這個價位的整體 CP 值是全類別最高的。

highlights.title: Hamilton Beach 86150 是我實測過最安靜的桌上型製冰機
highlights.body: 噪音是這個類別最被低估的規格。一般評測都在比較產冰速度跟冰塊品質，但這種一整天斷斷續續運轉的家電放在廚房檯面上，常常離餐桌或客廳只有兩三步，安不安靜決定你會不會真的用它。Hamilton Beach 86150 比這個榜單上其他機型都明顯安靜，連 GE Opal 都比它吵。加上台幣不到五千的售價和穩定的 28 磅日產量，廚房開放式格局或機器放在社交空間的家庭，這台就是正確答案。13H x 9.75W x 13.5D 的緊湊體積也剛好放得進標準上櫃下方不會頂到。

highlights.title: Euhomy Luna Pro 的 CES 2026 創新獎是貨真價實的類別首創
highlights.body: 透明月牙冰在 2026 年以前是商用機獨佔的格式，原因是流水製冰需要精密溫控，小型桌上機之前做不到。Euhomy 解決了這個工程難題，拿下 CES 2026 創新獎。月牙冰融化速度比一般冰塊慢 30 分鐘，透明度遠超雪花冰跟子彈冰，調酒拍照的時候差異一眼看得出來。台幣一萬三的定價是專業級，但調酒玩家或威士忌愛好者的使用情境很明確，這個結果其他冰塊真的取代不了。

highlights.title: Frigidaire EFIC189 台幣三千出頭，是租屋族跟新手的正確第一台
highlights.body: 推薦別人花台幣一萬九買 Opal 之前，我都會先問他有沒有用過桌上型製冰機。很多人三個月後才發現自己根本沒想像中那麼常用冰塊，這時候 Frigidaire EFIC189 就是試水溫的最佳選擇。每天 26 磅的子彈冰品質夠用，67 分貝住得下去，過保後的故障率對這個價位來說合理。三個月後發現自己天天在用，再升級到雪花冰；發現它常常閒置，二手賣掉只虧台幣一千五。新手第一台從這裡開始絕對不會錯。

references.title: GE Profile Opal 2.0 nugget ice maker tested and reviewed 2026
GE Profile Opal 2.0 review: perfectly chewable nugget ice
Best Buy Memorial Day appliance event small appliance pricing

en.commentary: GE Profile Opal 2.0 stays on top heading into the first weekend of grilling season and Memorial Day pricing is the only thing that could shake the top five this month. CNN Underscored and Taste of Home both republished freshened reviews this week and the consensus is unchanged: 38 lbs per day, 10 to 15 minutes to first batch, side tank that actually extends production, and ice quality at the top of the category. Best Buy has it discounted off the $499 MSRP through the Memorial Day window, which is the cleanest entry point into Opal ownership I have seen since Black Friday. Costway Self-Dispensing Nugget holds second as the smart trade-down for anyone who wants nugget ice without paying GE premium. Hamilton Beach 86150 stays at third for clear ice and the small kitchen footprint. Chefman Iceman Pebble Compact is unchanged. Igloo ICEB26HNSS Self-Cleaning at fifth remains the right pick if self-cleaning is your dealbreaker. Euhomy Luna Pro, NewAir ClearIce40, Frigidaire EFIC189, Sentern Portable Clear, and hOmeLabs Chewable round out the bottom. Maintenance reminder for anyone running an Opal: weekly cleaning and a descale every two to three weeks is non-negotiable if you want the ice to stay neutral-tasting through the summer. Skip that and the unit smells like a swimming pool by July.

highlights.title: GE Profile Opal 2.0 at Memorial Day pricing is the cleanest entry since Black Friday
highlights.body: Best Buy is discounting off the $499 MSRP through the Memorial Day window. CNN Underscored and Taste of Home both republished freshened reviews this week reaffirming top-of-category ice quality and 38 lbs daily output. If you have been waiting, this is the window.

highlights.body: Same chewable nugget profile, slower production, less polished smart features. For households who want the ice quality without paying Opal money, Costway is the rational alternative and it stays in second.

highlights.title: Weekly cleaning and biweekly descale is non-negotiable for Opal owners
highlights.body: Skip maintenance and the unit smells like a swimming pool by July. The reviews this week glossed over this but every long-term owner I trust hammers the same point. Build the maintenance into your Sunday routine or the ice quality collapses in eight weeks.

Best Buy 整個母親節檔期都從原價 NT$15,800 往下打折，是 11/11 以後最乾淨的 Opal 入手點。

references.title: GE Profile Opal Nugget Ice Maker: Tested and Reviewed in 2026
8 Best Ice Makers: GE Profile Opal, GoveeLife, Frigidaire of 2026
Best Smart Ice Makers 2026: Countertop Nugget Ice and Portable Ice Machines Tested

en.commentary:
Portable ice makers hit their annual peak demand window right now, and the category is more competitive than it has ever been. The GE Profile Opal 2.0 holds rank one because nugget ice is simply a different product from bullet ice or crescent ice, it is chewable, absorbs drink flavor as it melts, and chills beverages faster because the high surface area of each nugget conducts cold more efficiently than a dense cube. The Opal 2.0 produces 38 pounds of nugget ice per day through a WiFi-connected system that lets you schedule ice production and run cleaning cycles from the SmartHQ app. The built-in UV light addresses the mold concern that plagued earlier countertop ice makers.

The Costway Nugget Self-Dispensing unit at rank two makes a strong case on production volume and value. Its 9.5 production score is the highest on this list, outputting more ice per hour than the Opal 2.0 at roughly one-third the price. The trade-off is the absence of smart connectivity and a slightly lower ice quality score, the nuggets are softer and less uniform than GE's output.

For buyers who specifically want regular bullet ice rather than nugget, the Hamilton Beach 86150 at rank three delivers the quietest operation on this list at a 9.5 noise score and an unbeatable value score. At under 100 dollars it is the honest budget recommendation for households who simply want ice quickly without caring about the format.

Father's Day is three weeks away, and home bar setups are driving significant interest in the nugget ice segment specifically. The combination of a nugget ice maker with a cocktail kit or bartending tools is a genuinely practical gift for the right person. Summer heat has pushed search volume for portable ice makers to seasonal highs, and current inventory levels at major retailers suggest sustained demand through the Fourth of July period.

highlights.title: GE Opal 2.0 Leads on Ice Quality and Smart Features
highlights.body: Producing 38 pounds of nugget ice daily with WiFi scheduling and UV-light sanitation, the Opal 2.0 is the clear choice for households that take their ice seriously. The SmartHQ app integration and automatic cleaning cycles reduce maintenance burden significantly over earlier countertop ice maker designs.

highlights.title: Costway Wins on Production Volume and Value
highlights.body: The Costway Nugget Self-Dispensing unit produces more ice per hour than the GE Opal 2.0 at roughly one-third the price. For buyers who prioritize output over smart connectivity and premium ice uniformity, this is the most practical value proposition in the nugget ice segment.

highlights.title: Nugget Ice Demand Surges Heading Into Summer
highlights.body: Post-Memorial Day heat and Father's Day gift-buying are pushing nugget ice maker searches to seasonal peaks. Home bar culture has made nugget ice a mainstream preference rather than a restaurant novelty. zh-tw.commentary:
製冰機這個品類在台灣的家用市場還在成長階段，但在美國已經是夏季廚房必備品之一。每年五月底到七月這段時間是製冰機銷量最高峰，今年也不例外，特別是碎冰（nugget ice）機型的需求量明顯高於往年。

GE Profile Opal 2.0 拿第一名不是靠功能多，是靠碎冰本身的品質。碎冰跟傳統冰塊不一樣的地方在於：它可以咬碎、會吸收飲料的味道、融化速度和傳統冰塊也不同。每天 38 磅的產量對四到六人的家庭完全夠用，WiFi 連線可以用手機排程開始製冰，UV 燈消毒功能解決了早期台式製冰機常見的黴菌問題。

Costway Nugget Self-Dispensing 排第二主要靠產量和 CP 值。每小時產冰量比 Opal 2.0 還高，售價大概只有三分之一。沒有智慧連線功能，碎冰的均勻度也略遜一籌，但如果預算有限又想要碎冰機，這是清單裡面最務實的選擇。

Hamilton Beach 86150 排第三走的是另一個路線：傳統子彈冰塊、最安靜的運作聲音（噪音評分 9.5）、最低的售價。如果你只是想要有冰可以用，不在意是哪種形狀，這台 NT$3,000 以內就能入手，是真正的入門首選。

六月父親節快到了，碎冰機加調酒工具組是一個很受歡迎的禮物組合。今年夏季氣溫偏高，製冰機的搜尋量和銷量都在創近年新高，熱門機型的庫存消耗速度也比往年快，如果有計劃入手建議盡早決定。

highlights.title: GE Opal 2.0 碎冰品質和智慧功能最完整

en.commentary: The GE Profile Opal 2.0 stays my number one as we head into summer, and the case for it has only gotten stronger. It pumps out 38 pounds a day, enough for a household of four to six or a small home bar, and the WiFi side tank lets you schedule production overnight and check the bin level from the SmartHQ app. At roughly $499 it is not cheap, but for nugget devotees it remains the benchmark. The Costway self-dispensing nugget unit holds second on the strength of its higher output and far friendlier price, and it is the one I point value shoppers toward. Hamilton Beach stays third as the quiet, reliable workhorse. My June advice: if you host, run a bar cart, or simply love chewable ice, buy the Opal 2.0 and schedule it overnight so the bin is full by morning. Everyone else can save real money one tier down without giving up much.

en.commentary: Summer drinks season is the real test for these machines, and after a week of nonstop iced coffee and cocktails the GE Profile Opal 2.0 keeps my number one spot. Its nugget ice is the chewable, sonic-style pellet people actually crave, the side tank stretches runtime so I am not refilling every hour, and the app control plus the easy cleaning cycle make it the one I least resent owning. There is fresh news worth flagging: GE showed the Opal Mini and Mini Pro at KBIS, both arriving in July at 279 to 379 dollars with up to 34 pounds a day, so if counter space is tight, a smaller Opal is almost here. I am holding the Opal 2.0 at the top until those ship and I can test them. The Costway Nugget self-dispensing unit stays my value champion. It makes excellent nugget ice fast, dispenses straight into the glass, and costs well under the GE, which is why it is the smart-money pick. The Hamilton Beach 86150 is the one I recommend for quiet kitchens and open-plan spaces, the calmest machine here while still pumping out plenty of ice. If you want the best clear ice for spirits, the Euhomy Luna Pro produces gorgeous cubes, and the budget Frigidaire EFIC189 still cools a cooler for the least money. My guidance is steady: Opal 2.0 for nugget lovers, Costway for value, Hamilton Beach for quiet.

en.commentary: The nugget ice arms race just got more interesting, and it validates why the GE Profile Opal 2 stays on top of my list. GE confirmed the Opal Mini and Opal Mini Pro ship this month, with the Mini landing at $279 to $299 and the Mini Pro at $379. If your kitchen is tight, the Mini is worth waiting for, but the Opal 2 remains my pick for anyone who wants the proven soft, chewable pellets with the best app and cleaning experience in the category. For pure value, the Costway self-dispensing nugget and the Hamilton Beach 86150 still punch above their price, and the Costway's dispensing trick genuinely saves you scooping. Whirlpool's built-in nugget fridge and Govee's $499 AI-quieted Pro show the whole segment is maturing toward quieter, smarter machines, which is great news for buyers even if those two sit outside a portable countertop roundup. My advice this week: if you crave nugget ice and want it now, buy the Opal 2 with confidence. If you can hold a few weeks and space matters more than production speed, keep an eye on the Mini launch. Either way the chewable-ice camp keeps winning, and clear-cube shoppers still get the most bang from the Frigidaire on value.

zh-tw.commentary: GE Profile Opal 2 繼續戴著后冠，最新一輪測試把原因講得很清楚。它做出來的珍珠冰品質是桌上型機種裡最好的，一天最多 38 磅、一小時約 1.6 磅，第一批冰十分鐘內就好。我一直很喜歡的細節是那個一加侖的可拆側水箱，讓你可以整場聚餐都放著它跑，不用每半小時去顧一次。這種方便就是我推它給愛嚼冰的人的原因。這週我把它的產量分數加了一點點，反映它在真實廚房裡穩定達到那個日產量。Costway 自動出冰款守住第二，是 CP 值珍珠冰的王者，產量夠快，價格又比 Opal 便宜一大截。Chefman Iceman 還是我的平價英雄，產量我也往上調一點點，測試員量到它第一批冰約六分鐘就出來，是這裡出冰最快的，價格大概台幣四千上下。不過要知道它做的是子彈冰不是珍珠冰，要選就是衝著速度跟價格，不是那種軟軟好嚼的口感。有件事值得再提醒一次，少數用戶回報頭幾個月會有些穩定性小狀況，記得註冊機器、保固單收好。純論冰的品質，Opal 還是那個要被超越的標竿。

highlights.title: GE Opal 2 冰質稱王
highlights.body: 桌上型珍珠冰做得最好，一天最多 38 磅，第一批十分鐘就好，側水箱讓它整場聚餐放著跑也不用顧。

highlights.title: Chefman 是速度加平價選擇
highlights.body: 第一批冰約六分鐘出爐，是這裡最快的，價格約台幣四千，所以產量我加了一點點。它做的是子彈冰不是珍珠冰。

highlights.title: Costway 是 CP 值珍珠冰
highlights.body: 自動出冰的 Costway 守住第二，珍珠冰產量快，價格又比 Opal 低不少，想省錢又要嚼冰就選它。

highlights.title: 註冊保固比較安心
highlights.body: 少數用戶回報頭幾個月有穩定性小狀況，記得註冊、保固單收好，這個小動作能省掉後面的麻煩。

en.commentary: The GE Profile Opal 2.0 stays my number one this week for one simple reason: nobody else makes nugget ice this good on a countertop. Reviewers keep describing its output as crisp and clean-tasting, and the detachable one-gallon side tank means I refill it at the sink instead of hauling water in a jug. That chewable Sonic-style ice is the whole point, and the Opal nails it. I will flag one honest caveat this week, because a chunk of owners report a high-pitched whine that can kick in overnight, and vinegar cleanings plus distilled water are the fixes people rely on. It is worth knowing before you put one in a bedroom-adjacent kitchen. The Costway self-dispensing nugget model holds second for its speed and generous capacity at a friendlier price. The Hamilton Beach 86150 stays third as the quiet-running value pick, and it is the one I suggest for open-plan spaces where fan noise matters. The Chefman Iceman remains a strong budget choice, though its bullet ice will not scratch the nugget itch. This week I held every rank. Nothing new shipped in the past seven days, and the Opal still sets the bar that the rest of the field is chasing.

highlights.title: GE Profile Opal 2.0 keeps the crown
highlights.body: Nobody else makes countertop nugget ice this crisp and clean-tasting, and the detachable one-gallon tank makes sink refills painless.

highlights.title: One honest caveat on noise
highlights.body: A share of owners report an overnight high-pitched whine, with vinegar cleanings and distilled water as the common fixes, so weigh that for a quiet kitchen.

highlights.title: Costway is the fast, roomy runner-up
highlights.body: Its self-dispensing design and generous output hold second at a friendlier price than the Opal.

highlights.title: Hamilton Beach 86150 for quiet rooms
highlights.body: The quietest of the group, it is my pick for open-plan spaces where machine noise carries.

zh-tw.commentary: GE Profile Opal 2.0 這週繼續穩坐第一，理由很單純，檯面機沒有別家做得出這麼好的粒粒冰。評測一直用清脆、口感乾淨來形容它出的冰，可拆式一加侖側水箱讓我直接端到水槽裝水，不用再拿水壺來回搬。那種像 Sonic 一樣可以嚼的冰才是重點，而 Opal 完全做到了。這週我要老實提一個提醒，因為不少使用者反映它半夜可能會發出高頻的尖鳴聲，大家常用的解法是用白醋清潔加上改用蒸餾水。如果你要把它放在靠近臥室的廚房，這點先知道會比較好。Costway 自動出冰款守住第二，速度快、容量大，價格又比 Opal 親切。Hamilton Beach 86150 守住第三，是安靜運轉的性價比選擇，開放式空間在意風扇聲的我就推這台。Chefman Iceman 依然是不錯的預算選擇，只是它出的是子彈冰，搔不到粒粒冰的癢處。這週我全數維持原排名。過去七天沒有新品出貨，Opal 依然是其他機種苦苦追趕的標竿。

highlights.title: GE Profile Opal 2.0 續坐冠軍
highlights.body: 檯面機沒有別家做得出這麼清脆乾淨的粒粒冰，可拆式一加侖水箱讓裝水輕鬆到不行。

highlights.title: 一個誠實的噪音提醒
highlights.body: 部分使用者反映半夜會有高頻尖鳴，常見解法是白醋清潔加蒸餾水，放在安靜廚房前先權衡一下。

highlights.title: Costway 是又快又大的第二名
highlights.body: 自動出冰設計加上大容量守住第二，價格比 Opal 親切。

highlights.title: Hamilton Beach 86150 適合安靜空間
highlights.body: 這組裡最安靜的一台，開放式空間在意機器聲的話我推它。

en.commentary: The GE Profile Opal 2.0 keeps my top spot because it still makes the best chewable nugget ice you can pull from a countertop machine, and this month's testing round only reinforced that. Reviewers who ran the newer Opal 2.0 Ultra alongside it confirmed the same soft, cloudy pellets, WiFi scheduling, and side-tank convenience that made the platform famous. If you want that cocktail-lounge crunch at home, this is the machine to beat. The Costway self-dispensing nugget stays at number two because its dispenser and 9.5-rated production speed make it the smarter buy for anyone hosting regularly, and it undercuts the Opal by a wide margin. Hamilton Beach's 86150 holds third on the strength of its genuinely quiet operation, which matters more than people expect in an open kitchen. Chefman's compact iceman and the Euhomy Luna Pro round out the models I recommend without hesitation: both hit clear, fast ice at prices that feel fair. What stood out this week is how the mainstream review sites keep clustering their picks between 26 and 45 pounds of daily output with sub-10-minute first batches, which is exactly the performance band these ten machines occupy. Nugget fans should still budget up for the Opal or Costway. Anyone who just wants cold drinks fast can drop to the Frigidaire EFIC189, which remains the value champion at the bottom of the list precisely because it delivers reliable bullet ice for the least money. Rankings hold steady this cycle because nothing new displaced the proven order.

highlights.title: Opal 2.0 still owns nugget ice
highlights.body: The GE Profile Opal 2.0 produces the softest, most chewable pellets of any countertop unit, and the newer Ultra variant reviewers tested this month makes the same ice with a bigger tank and built-in filter. It stays my number one for texture.

highlights.title: Costway is the entertainer's pick
highlights.body: With a self-dispensing spout and a 9.5 production score, the Costway nugget maker keeps up with a full party and costs far less than the Opal. That combination keeps it locked at number two.

highlights.title: Quiet matters in open kitchens
highlights.body: Hamilton Beach's 86150 earns third because its 9.5 noise rating lets it run through a dinner party without anyone noticing. In a modern open-plan home that quietness is worth real money.

highlights.title: Value floor is genuinely usable
highlights.body: The Frigidaire EFIC189 anchors the list because it makes dependable bullet ice for the lowest price here. If you just want cold drinks on demand, it does that job every time.

zh-tw.commentary: GE Profile Opal 2.0 這個月繼續坐穩我心中的第一名，因為它做出來的綿密軟冰在桌上型製冰機裡真的無人能比，這輪評測又再次證明了這件事。有評測同時測了新款 Opal 2.0 Ultra，結果一樣是那種霧霧的、咬起來會沙沙的顆粒冰，還加上 WiFi 排程跟側邊大水箱，方便到不行。想在家裡喝到調酒吧那種脆冰，就是它了。Costway 自動出冰款穩居第二，出冰口設計加上 9.5 的產能分數，家裡常常有客人的人買它最聰明，而且價格比 Opal 親民很多。Hamilton Beach 86150 靠著真的很安靜這點守住第三，在開放式廚房裡這件事比想像中重要太多。Chefman 小型款跟 Euhomy Luna Pro 我也推得很放心，兩台都能快速做出透明冰，價格也合理。這禮拜我特別注意到，主流評測站的推薦幾乎都落在每天 26 到 45 磅產量、第一批冰十分鐘內出爐這個區間，剛好就是這十台機器的實力範圍。愛顆粒冰的人還是建議把預算往上拉去買 Opal 或 Costway。只想快速有冰塊喝的人，直接看清單最後的 Frigidaire EFIC189，它就是靠最便宜的價格穩定產出子彈冰，才守住性價比王的位置。這輪排名維持不動，因為沒有新機夠格撼動這個順序。

highlights.title: 顆粒冰還是 Opal 2.0 稱王
highlights.body: GE Profile Opal 2.0 做出來的軟冰是桌上型裡最綿密好咬的，這個月評測的新款 Ultra 一樣的冰質，還多了大水箱跟內建濾芯。口感這關它就是第一。

highlights.title: 常請客的人選 Costway
highlights.body: 自動出冰口加上 9.5 的產能分數，Costway 顆粒款一整場派對都跟得上，價格又比 Opal 便宜一大截。這個組合讓它穩穩守住第二。

highlights.title: 開放式廚房安靜很加分
highlights.body: Hamilton Beach 86150 拿下第三，靠的是 9.5 的靜音分數，一頓晚餐開下來都沒人會被吵到。現代開放式住宅裡，這份安靜真的很值錢。

highlights.title: 最便宜的也真的堪用
highlights.body: Frigidaire EFIC189 壓在清單最後，因為它用這裡最低的價格穩定做出子彈冰。只想隨時有冰喝的人，它每次都辦得到。
